stat_of_week: .875 - Sal Stewart posted a .875 OBP for the Albuquerque Bombers, the most absurd on-base mark of the young season and a number that almost defies belief at any level of baseball
thriller_teams: General Hospital vs Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs
thriller_note: A dead-even tie decided by razor-thin margins in nearly every category — General Hospital's elite OBP and RBI edge was perfectly cancelled out by the Wonderdogs' pitching and power, leaving both teams with a half-win that feels like a full loss heading into a brutal stretch ahead.
key_storyline: The Benchwarmers pulled off a 7–3 win over the Buttville Skidmarks despite posting just 2 HR, a 4.50 ERA, and zero quality starts — surviving almost entirely on Zach Neto's 6 runs and a trio of infielders padding the R and RBI columns. Meanwhile Buttville's Chase DeLauter launched 4 HR and Kevin Gausman posted a Cy Young-caliber line (11 K, 0.17 WHIP) in a losing effort, making this one of the ugliest luck-driven outcomes of Week 1. The Skidmarks have real weapons but simply cannot get out of their own way when it counts.
lucky_reason: They won 7–3 despite zero quality starts, a 4.50 ERA, only 2 HR, and a .314 OBP — their run total and category wins vastly outpaced the underlying quality of their performance against a Buttville squad with superior pitching and power.
unlucky_reason: Chase DeLauter's 4 HR and Kevin Gausman's 0.17 WHIP were genuinely elite performances that deserved a win, but a 6.02 team ERA dragged them to a 3–7 loss against a Benchwarmers squad they should have beaten.
records_broken: General Hospital broke their own season record for highest team OBP, posting .414 this week to surpass the previous mark — also .414, set by General Hospital in Week 1, meaning the record holds and was matched exactly.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Nine category wins, 8 HR, 38 K, and four hitters with OBP above .385 — this offense is operating on a different level and the pitching held up; still the class of the league | same

2. BaseballPro77 | An 8–2 demolition powered by Murakami's 3 HR, Trout's 2 HR and .650 OBP, and Stanton adding pop — the rotation is uneven but the offensive ceiling remains the highest in the league | up

3. General Hospital | A 4–4 tie stings but Brandon Lowe's 3 HR, Luis Robert Jr.'s .571 OBP, and Cam Schlittler's 0.19 WHIP confirm this is an elite two-way roster that ran into a buzzsaw and still nearly won | same

4. Albuquerque Bombers | A 6–4 win built on pitching — Alcantara and Rogers both threw quality starts with sub-1.00 WHIPs — and Sal Stewart's .875 OBP is the kind of absurdity that wins tight categories; four waiver adds add depth | up

5. North Correa | A clean 7–3 win with Arozarena scoring 5 runs, Bregman going deep twice, and Sale and Glasnow both delivering quality starts — this rotation is starting to look like a genuine strength | same

6. Athletic Park Dragons | A 5–4 win over Hiroshima masked by a 4.10 ERA and thin offense; Cristopher Sánchez's 0.50 WHIP and Joe Ryan's 0.56 WHIP are elite, but the lineup needs more consistent run production | same

7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Six HR, five SB, and 43 K from the pitching staff in a 4–6 loss — the raw talent is undeniable but a 6.00 team ERA with zero quality starts is a rotation-wide emergency | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | Tarik Skubal's 0.50 WHIP and 44 team strikeouts are genuine weapons, but a 4–5 loss despite a 2.35 ERA tells you the offense is not yet pulling its weight | same

9. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A 4–4 tie with seven HR and solid pitching but still no win — Brendan Donovan's 2 HR and .556 OBP were excellent and Chase Dollander's addition adds rotation intrigue, but the luck demons persist | same

10. Riverside Roughnecks | A brutal 1–9 loss despite Shea Langeliers' 3 HR, Royce Lewis going deep twice, and Jacob Misiorowski's 11 K — a 7.91 team ERA with zero quality starts is simply unsalvageable | down

11. Canberra Wombats | Dylan Cease's 12 K and 1.69 ERA were wasted in a 2–8 blowout; Logan Webb's 10.80 ERA and zero quality starts as a team indicate a rotation that needs an emergency overhaul | same

12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Bryan Woo and Gregory Soto both impressed but Matthew Boyd's 14.73 ERA torpedoed the staff to a 3–7 loss; dropping McGreevy without a real replacement signals a roster still searching for answers | same

13. Benchwarmers | The 7–3 win is real but the underlying stats — no QS, 4.50 ERA, 2 HR — scream regression; Zach Neto carried this team and the rotation looks thin with Sheehan posting a 10.80 ERA | down

14. Buttville Skidmarks | DeLauter's 4 HR and Gausman's near-perfect outing deserved far better than a 3–7 loss; with a 6.02 team ERA and wildly inconsistent rotation, the talent keeps getting wasted by pitching volatility | same
